examples: It was compressed enough to become a protostar but enough material was left over to form planets, moons, asteroids and comets.
The conditions in a protostar resemble far more closely in situation in red giants, most of whose volume is caught up in stormy convection.
The simulation reveals how pre-stellar gases would have actually evolved under the simpler physics of the early universe to form this protostar.
The scientists were also able to identify the object as a protostar that is still in the process of forming.
